subject: First Year Algebra
instructions: Answer 4 questions. Write your solutions clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

problems.text: Let \(G\) be a group of order \(2022=2\cdot 3\cdot 337\) (as a product of primes).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that \(G\) has a normal subgroup of index \(2\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Is there a group of order \(2022\) with trivial center?

problems.text: Let \(G\) be a finite group of order \(p^a\), where \(p\) is a prime and \(a\) is a positive integer. Prove that if \(N\) is a nontrivial normal subgroup of \(G\), then \(N\cap Z(G)\ne\{1\}\).
Prove that if a group \(G\) has a proper subgroup of finite index, then it has a proper normal subgroup of finite index.
Let \(G\) be the additive group of rational numbers.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that \(G\) cannot be written as the direct product of two proper subgroups.

problems.text: Give the definition of a solvable (finite) group. (You may give any of the equivalent definitions.) Prove, using your definition, that the symmetric group on four letters is solvable.
Let \(G\) be a finite group and \(p\) a prime. Let \(K\) be the subgroup of \(G\) generated by all elements of order relatively prime to \(p\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that \(K\) is a characteristic subgroup of \(G\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that \(|G/K|\) is a power of \(p\).
